# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from 'https://deno.land/std@0.107.0/http/server.ts';
import { openKv } from 'https://deno.land/x/kv/mod.ts';

const kv = openKv('users');

const server = serve({ port: 8080 });
console.log(`HTTP webserver running on: http://localhost:8080/`);

for await (const request of server) {
  if (request.method === 'GET' && request.url.startsWith('/search')) {
    const url = new URL(request.url, 'http://localhost');
    const userId = url.searchParams.get('userId')
    const filterParam = url.searchParams.get('filterParam')

    if (!userId || !filterParam) {
      request.respond({ status: 400, body: JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid Query Parameters' }) });
      continue;
    }

    const key = ['users', userId, filterParam].join(':');
    const value = await kv.get(key)

    if (!value) {
      request.respond({ status: 404, body: JSON.stringify({ error: 'Resource Not Found' }) });
      continue;
    }

    request.respond({ body: JSON.stringify({ key, value })});
  } else {
    request.respond({ status: 404, body: JSON.stringify({ error: 'Route Not Found' }) });
  }
}
